submission.questionnaireText:
Bryant Grizzle
Adult 04/30/1997 (28)  Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
10/01/2025 at 3:35 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Around the belly
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
Not Applicable
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
Both
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
Seemingly no reason
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
No
11. Do you NOT dream?
(3) This is a bothersome issue for me
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
Not Applicable
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
Not Applicable
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
Yes
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
No
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
Yes
Vinegar, sometimes salt, milk, carbonated drinks, lunch meat lately , sometimes sugar/juice
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
Yes
On and off anxiety/timidness. Much worse at this low point now almost like panic feelings over little things
34. Do you experience constipation?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
35. Do you experience nausea?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
39. Do you experience belching?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
Yes
Fatty foods, cows milk, high fructose corn syrup, sometimes starches (white rice and potatoes). I think goat milk fat is more fine
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(1) I do not experience this
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Sometimes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
Taurine: helps liver/gallbladder pain 1000mg x2 daily
Ox bile/Tudca: similar just grants a little relief from liver gallbladder issue
Cascara for constipation
Intenzyme from pure encapsulation: helps with gallbladder liver soreness
Too low fat in diet= not good
Too much =problem
Heavy starchy problem for liver/GB
But heavy fructose makes me dizzy so I try to mix and match
Oregano oil
Undecylenic acid
Monolaurin
Camphosal
All of these were mixed results; never resolved issue. Oregano/camphosal messsed up my sleep pretty bad. Could not fall asleep on them
Functional doc started a Nystatin course last week but only 1X500k unit daily: no change so far
Activated charcoal/ cholestyramine help some with symptoms
Aspirin 325mg: messes with sleep but no other effects other than knocking out a headache
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
Benfothiamine pure bulk; 200-300. Felt like it was helping at first with energy and constipation but doesn’t feel like it does anything now.
I was taking TTFD and Thismine HCL high dose for a period of time and it also felt good for a couple days then stopped
Magnesium glycinate 400 mg 1x daily(Doublewood supps)
B complex pure encapsulations;
Both of these I have taken for months and don’t really notice anything from them anymore. I stopped the complex because it’s too stimulating in my low state for some reason
Taurine: (now powder) ; liver gallbladder support 1000mgx2 daily
Vitamin C Acerola cherry (health force super foods); taking this for gallbladder/adrenal support. It’s newer and I take 6 caps across the day. 374mg vit C
Intenzyme (pure encapsulations)digestive enzyme one with dinner; helps with gallbladder soreness
Betaine HCL w/ pepsin (natures life) 3x caps with full meal
Tudca/ox bile. (Lifeblud) 1 cap daily
Better bitters (Herb Farm) 3x daily pre meals 3 pumps
Vit d (premier research) 2-5k is daily
Vit k mk 4(forefront health) 10 mg in the morning for a couple months bc it was helping with stress. Now just 2mg in the morning with the D
Vitamin E when I eat out. (A.C. grace high gamma mixed toco) unique e. 1 soft gel
My functional doc gave me a meters cocktail IV drip this week and last week . I also got two b12 shots earlier this year
Hormones; not taking any currently, but was taking
Progesterone 1 drop progestE on nights when I couldn’t sleep. There were periods this last year where I would be doing this regularly for a few weeks
Pregnenolone (health natura)100mg oral: on stressful days sometimes used for multiple days in a row. The last time I took 100mg at once is when the wheels came off and I got even more dizzy/nauseous than I had been.
Androsterone 1-3mg topical (idea labs): felt good at first and made my fingers stop hurting so I could rock climb again but I felt weird. Unstable endurance wise and my heart felt funny. I only did this for a week or two then started getting dizzy and nauseated
NDT (thyrovanz) 25mg one in the morning. Sometimes this gave me energy, sometimes stress w/high heart rate and disrupted sleep. Eventually it just started making me dizzy. Couldn’t reliably go higher in dose
T3 (ideal labs and blue sky) 1-8mcg have been tried. I felt good at first using this regularly then started getting overstimulated on lower and lower doses. It also makes me dizzy now so I stopped
Not taking any hormones now
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
Yes
Nasal spray 1x each nostril (edta, itraconazole, mupirocin, triancinolone)
This is new and only a few days in. Functional dr prescribed bc my nose is inflamed inside
Nystatin 1x 500k units
Ondansetron for nausea 4-8mg as needed but makes the dizziness worse so I try not to use it
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
Yes
CIRS is the only diagnosis I have received in any “official” capacity in 2023
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
Beef, lunch meat, shrimp, scallops, oysters(canned 1 every 2 weeks), eggs occasionally

Grapes, oranges when I can get good ones, dried figs,

Juice (simply nature Super food organic 100%juice)

Cheese A2 when possible, goat cheese, goat milk whole, sometime Skyr yogurt nonfat, ice cream occasionally

Homemade hash browns, green beans,

Potato chips(olive oil), corn chips(avocado),

White rice

Glass cokes(stopped bc of caffeine for now), ginger ale for nausea and carbonation + calories(when I wasn’t able to keep much down)

Tastes cookies gluten free

I was doing my wife’s sourdough and store bought for a period of time but cut it out bc I didn’t know if it was causing me to feel bad

Homemade gelatin gummies with juice

Sweetened dark chocolate (enjoy life)

Occasional carrot salad

Spring and filtered water at home but I don’t drink it that often, sometimes coconut water

I eat out infrequently but if we do eat out it’s Mexican, In n out or a new bowls place that says they don’t use seed oils

Calories was probably 2000-2500 a day pre this wave of dizziness nausea and fatigue. Now I’m probably getting in 1600-1800 on a good day with some days much lower.

My last full chronometer day was in a July and I ate 2500; a little over half carbs , a little more than 1/4 fat and the rest protein( 356 g carbs, 97 G protein, 84bgbfat)

That’s probably pretty normal. I have drifted lower and lower in protein over the last 6-8 months —maybe stomach acid related. Some days fat is probably much higher.

Typically low pufa other than the chips

Sugar+fruit sources of carbs is probably higher than starches in a day most days

I haven’t been hungry for breakfast in a year or so but still try to eat a little. Moderate lunch, bigger dinner, and snacks here and there usually something pre bed.

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
I have been trying to rockclimb once or twice a week but that’s new. Last couple months was sedentary before other than afternoon walks after work. Last couple weeks no exercise in the low state

I work as a broker for Schwab and don’t have time away from my desk and too tired for standing desk. The job does stresss me out but it’s less stressful now than it was a year ago. I was a background painter in animation but got let go during strikes in LA and had to pivot. I was remote and have been in Texas for the time I worked for that co.

My current jobs is mostly in office with some work from home.

Very little time outside unfortunately

My wife and I have also been leading a group at our church for other young families and that we have done for a couple years. It has been tiring and we are about to stop.

Overall I have been too stressed

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
I have felt low/fatigued for years with various pains and other weird symptoms. I would love to just feel normal

I have had lightheaded/dizziness for years particular when I am nervous or stressed and it has been a major impediment in life.

I fatigue easily and can’t do what I would like to.

I learned about beriberi earlier this year and it means “I can’t I can’t” and that has been me. No more energy to pull up on my bootstraps to do something.

In the immediate I am just wanting to be able to eat like normal and not be intensely dizzy. Baby comes at the end of this month (hopefully) so being at least stable enough to drive and not get tired doing dishes would be great

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
No but I did get some kind of stomach bug or maybe Covid in 2022 and have had gallbladder pain on and off since then.

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
5.11. 193lbs. Pant waist size 36

I gained like 35-40 lbs from like nov/dec. 24 - march april 25.

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
I gained like 35-40 lbs from like nov/dec. 24 - march april 25.

I could tell something changed in the nov/dec timeframe bc I had been feeling much better. The world had color I had energy, even thoug work was stressful with new job and training I still felt good. Then idk if I expanded my carbs too quickly into straight up sugars like coke or it was adding sourdough but I had a hard pivot. Energy dropped, joy dropped and the weight gain stated. Maybe it was coming regardless of those changes, but I told my wife one night after I got a sourdough pizza from a. Neapolitan pizza place near us that something shifted lol.

For a brief timeline I am 28. From probably 18-26,27 i had anxiety fatigue joint pain, weird vision stuff a lot of random things, skinny fatish. Eventually tried fasting then carnivore because all the docs I had seen didn’t help and we had spent a lot. That made me feel good at first then terrible. I did that for 9 months 2022-2023. Gallbladder issues started (or became noticeable) in there after getting a stomach bug or Covid. Then I found CIRS chronic inflammatory response syndrome and got tested. Did the protocol warts and all. Moved to a new rental house we had tested for mold, got on cholestyramine and eventually VIP and felt much better. I had slowly shifted to low carb in this time as well. Then after I stopped working with my CIRS dic(their clinic flooded and they closed) I got into bioenergetics and started lowering protein and increasing carbs from mostly fruit and juice w/ some starch. Felt like the world was golden for a time, then started having some breathing issues and thiamine helped that stop. And I had a period of time where I had a huge appetite 3500-4000 calories w/morning hunger. That tapered off and then I had the dip eventually that I mentioned before. I also had a job loss in end of 2023 (stressful time) and my father in law passed unexpectedly last year. Both pretty big stressors and this job is not where I wanna be long term but is survivable now that I know the ropes.
submission.takehomeText:
Bryant Grizzle
Adult 04/30/1997 (28)  Schedule appointment  Edit

3. Take Home Assessment Inputs (reference PDF)
10/05/2025 at 1:13 PM
Note



What are your four resting heart beats per minute? Please separate values with commas. [example: 78, 81, 75, 80]
Afternoon: 78,80, 76, 70

How many times did you urinate each day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 6, 6, 8, 7]
5, 5, 5, 5

On average, how foamy was your urine?
(2) Somewhat foamy
How many times did you defecate per day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 3, 1, 0, 2]
2, 2, 3, 2

What Types on the Bristol Stool Scale did each of your bowel movements fall under? Please separate values with commas. [example: Type 2, Type 2, Type 4, Type 5, Type 7, Type 1, Type 6]
Please describe any other abnormalities of the stool observed, such as altered color or undigested food in the stool.
5-6, 5-6, 4-5, 4, 4, 4-5,6, 4, 5-6

It alternated between yellow brown with some floating stool to dark brown green. Never normal solid regular brown—but close to it one time. I’m taking taurine and Tudca, so that might be why the darker green brown

I also threw up from nausea on day 3 in the morning

What were both of your temperature measurements each morning? Please separate values with commas. [example:
98.2, 98.4]

Temperature:
96.53, 97.57, 96.87, 97.59

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did your last period begin? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did you take your first temperature measurement? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.
submission.advisorNotesText:
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
3:00 PM – 4:00 PM
Progress Note 
Bryant is an interesting case. It seems to me he’s had lifelong anxiety and I couldn’t really get a reason why this may have started, but there were a few things that piqued my interest. He did say this started in his late teens and as I asked more questions it did fall around the same time that he went to a trip to South Africa (this involved multiple vaccines). As you read more of the history, I’m also tuned into a possible parasite. He was in South Africa and also in Mexico in his early 20’s. It seems to correlate to when this all started. His symptoms seem more heavy metal toxicity/vaccine injury to me, but I don’t want to rule out a parasite given some of his other digestive complaints.

His main symptom is generated from poor digestion. This started again in his late teens/early 20’s and worsened over time. In 2022 he got a bad flu and never recovered after that. Since then he’s had ongoing gallbladder sx’s, upper quadrant pain, burping, gas, distention, fat intolerance etc. Classic digestive issues that we deal with daily.

This case was hard to follow, because he’s done a lot of different things along the way and had a lot of testing. Most recently ran a trial of nystatin, but said that he felt like it didn’t do much, maybe made him worse. He also felt like the doc he was working with didn’t know what he was doing. That’s when he reached out to us I believe.

Overall, this kid has been driving the struggle bus. No matter what he’s done, he can’t get relief. He tried low carb and although his gut was better, he said the brain fog/low energy was unbearable. Then he at some carbs/sugar and it was like he came back to life initially, but then everything started bothering him. Most recently he feels specifically like sugar/sucrose/fructose is bothering him. It seems that no matter what he does, he can’t get relief. Higher fat bothers his gallbladder, low fat leaves him with brain fog and gas.

He’s tried things like B1, which gives temporary relief. Same with thyroid and any other hormones like pregnenalone. He takes them and for a few days has a window of relief and then everything comes rushing back. Another interesting thing that happened was that he took zinc recently and it made him really sick and his whole side over his spleen/pancreas broke out in a rash. I found this interesting that he broke out over his organs like that.

So in a nutshell, this kid isn’t tolerating food or many supplements. I feel like he is one of those cases where either traveling or vaccines toxified him and he’s simply never recovered and now he’s really struggling with his digestion. He is also currently struggling with nausea and dizziness. This has been the recent exacerbation that has him out of work currently due to nausea and dizziness and sounds like even some vomiting at times.

Also to note that he has noticed that the back of his tongue has been dark brown. We are certainly dealing with an overgrowth here. This kid has pretty much tried everything under the sun for bile flow and digestion. Given the neurological sx’s he’s having, the dizziness and brain fog, I think he’s dealing with significant vitamin deficiencies as well and specifically B vitamins.

Lastly, he did the CIRS protocol. (where is the eye-roll emoji) He’s done cholestyramine and some other detoxes. I’m sure this just wrecked his biome even more.

Supplements he’s tried/currently taking:

Benfothiamine pure bulk; 200-300. Felt like it was helping at first with energy and constipation but doesn’t feel like it does anything now.
I was taking TTFD and Thismine HCL high dose for a period of time and it also felt good for a couple days then stopped
Magnesium glycinate 400 mg 1x daily(Doublewood supps)
B complex pure encapsulations;
Both of these I have taken for months and don’t really notice anything from them anymore. I stopped the complex because it’s too stimulating in my low state for some reason
Taurine: (now powder) ; liver gallbladder support 1000mgx2 daily
Vitamin C Acerola cherry (health force super foods); taking this for gallbladder/adrenal support. It’s newer and I take 6 caps across the day. 374mg vit C
Intenzyme (pure encapsulations)digestive enzyme one with dinner; helps with gallbladder soreness
Betaine HCL w/ pepsin (natures life) 3x caps with full meal
Tudca/ox bile. (Lifeblud) 1 cap daily
Better bitters (Herb Farm) 3x daily pre meals 3 pumps
Vit d (premier research) 2-5k is daily
Vit k mk 4(forefront health) 10 mg in the morning for a couple months bc it was helping with stress. Now just 2mg in the morning with the D
Vitamin E when I eat out. (A.C. grace high gamma mixed toco) unique e. 1 soft gel
My functional doc gave me a meters cocktail IV drip this week and last week . I also got two b12 shots earlier this year
Hormones; not taking any currently, but was taking
Progesterone 1 drop progestE on nights when I couldn’t sleep. There were periods this last year where I would be doing this regularly for a few weeks
Pregnenolone (health natura)100mg oral: on stressful days sometimes used for multiple days in a row. The last time I took 100mg at once is when the wheels came off and I got even more dizzy/nauseous than I had been.
Androsterone 1-3mg topical (idea labs): felt good at first and made my fingers stop hurting so I could rock climb again but I felt weird. Unstable endurance wise and my heart felt funny. I only did this for a week or two then started getting dizzy and nauseated
NDT (thyrovanz) 25mg one in the morning. Sometimes this gave me energy, sometimes stress w/high heart rate and disrupted sleep. Eventually it just started making me dizzy. Couldn’t reliably go higher in dose
T3 (ideal labs and blue sky) 1-8mcg have been tried. I felt good at first using this regularly then started getting overstimulated on lower and lower doses. It also makes me dizzy now so I stopped
Not taking any hormones now
Goals:

I have felt low/fatigued for years with various pains and other weird symptoms. I would love to just feel normal

I have had lightheaded/dizziness for years particular when I am nervous or stressed and it has been a major impediment in life.

I fatigue easily and can’t do what I would like to.

I learned about beriberi earlier this year and it means “I can’t I can’t” and that has been me. No more energy to pull up on my bootstraps to do something.

In the immediate I am just wanting to be able to eat like normal and not be intensely dizzy. Baby comes at the end of this month (hopefully) so being at least stable enough to drive and not get tired doing dishes would be great

Other info to note:

I gained like 35-40 lbs from like nov/dec. 24 - march april 25.

I could tell something changed in the nov/dec timeframe bc I had been feeling much better. The world had color I had energy, even thoug work was stressful with new job and training I still felt good. Then idk if I expanded my carbs too quickly into straight up sugars like coke or it was adding sourdough but I had a hard pivot. Energy dropped, joy dropped and the weight gain stated. Maybe it was coming regardless of those changes, but I told my wife one night after I got a sourdough pizza from a. Neapolitan pizza place near us that something shifted lol.

For a brief timeline I am 28. From probably 18-26,27 i had anxiety fatigue joint pain, weird vision stuff a lot of random things, skinny fatish. Eventually tried fasting then carnivore because all the docs I had seen didn’t help and we had spent a lot. That made me feel good at first then terrible. I did that for 9 months 2022-2023. Gallbladder issues started (or became noticeable) in there after getting a stomach bug or Covid. Then I found CIRS chronic inflammatory response syndrome and got tested. Did the protocol warts and all. Moved to a new rental house we had tested for mold, got on cholestyramine and eventually VIP and felt much better. I had slowly shifted to low carb in this time as well. Then after I stopped working with my CIRS dic(their clinic flooded and they closed) I got into bioenergetics and started lowering protein and increasing carbs from mostly fruit and juice w/ some starch. Felt like the world was golden for a time, then started having some breathing issues and thiamine helped that stop. And I had a period of time where I had a huge appetite 3500-4000 calories w/morning hunger. That tapered off and then I had the dip eventually that I mentioned before. I also had a job loss in end of 2023 (stressful time) and my father in law passed unexpectedly last year. Both pretty big stressors and this job is not where I wanna be long term but is survivable now that I know the ropes.

This kid is a mess. We need to go easy with him, but he needs some measures to clear his gut. I really don’t think it’s a parasite, but that’s in the back of my head if some of these other things don’t work. He has had a lot of travel to odd places, but his WBC counts were ok.

Plan:

Antibiotics: I think minocycline here for sure.

Tributyrin

Lactorferrin

After 2 weeks of the above, let’s get him on lactobacillus plantarum from bioptimizers.

Liquid Anderson minerals: Start with 1/2 dose due to his reaction to zinc.

Topical A,D,K

I’m nervous about B vitamins, given his sensitivity. Can we do liquid B’s? I like Forefront Health for the sensitive ones!

Diet: I’m addressing this with him over messenger, but we need to find 5 or 6 foods that he tolerates and stick with this while we correct his gut and then introduce things ONE AT A TIME!
submission.daltonsFinalNotes:
Parasitology from Precision point

Mycotoxin test

eliminate PUFA from report

iron panel

glutamine

also note he has brown coating on tongue which is also indicative of same thing as white coat
